summary: Added catch/error/retry and an explicit loading state to the Insights history load in src/features/insights/InsightsScreen.tsx. Previously the focus-effect called getInsightsViewModel(...).then(...) with no .catch, so a repository read failure (repo.getEventsInRange -> loadEventsInRange -> getInsightsViewModel) became an unhandled promise rejection and silently fell back to the empty 'Getting started' view model, misrepresenting a load failure as 'no data' with no way to recover. I introduced a small load-status machine ('loading' | 'ready' | 'error'). The load logic was extracted into a stable `load` useCallback (called on tab focus and by the Retry button) that uses a monotonic request-token ref so a slow load resolving after blur or after a retry is ignored, and a loadedRef so stale-data fallback doesn't add the view model to the effect deps (which would loop). On first-load failure with no prior data it shows a calm alert-tinted error card ('Couldn't load insights' + reassurance that logs are safe) with an accessible 'Try again' pill that re-runs the load; a refresh failure while data is already on screen keeps the last good view model instead of flashing an error. While the very first load is in flight it now shows a calm loading card (ActivityIndicator + 'Gathering your last 7 days…') instead of prematurely rendering the empty state. All UI reuses existing theme tokens (colors, fonts, radii, shadows, surfaces) and matches the existing intro/section card patterns. The genuine empty state (dataDays === 0 'Getting started') and existing analytics/milestone behavior are unchanged. No architecture rewrite, no new deps, no git operations.
validation_notes: Ran 'npx tsc --noEmit' — clean (no errors). Ran 'npx eslint src/features/insights/InsightsScreen.tsx' — 0 problems (an earlier react-hooks/exhaustive-deps warning from a reloadNonce approach was eliminated by refactoring to the shared `load` callback + request-token ref). package.json exposes only a 'lint' script (expo lint); there is no 'typecheck' or 'test' script and no pre-existing test for InsightsScreen, so no unit test was added. The dev-only console.warn on failure uses console.warn intentionally (real error path, not an expected state) and is guarded by __DEV__.
manual_followups: Optional: manually QA the error path by forcing repo.getEventsInRange to reject (or running with corrupted local storage) to confirm the error card + Try again re-load flows on device.
Optional: if desired later, the loading/error cards could be extracted into shared components if other read-backed screens need the same treatment.
apple_review_risk_remaining: Local reads from device storage rarely fail, so the error path is a defensive safety net rather than a common state; it is exercised only on genuine storage read failures.
